differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune encephalitis (LGI1 or other VGKC-complex antibody)
Alzheimer's disease with concurrent cerebrovascular disease
Metastatic malignancy to the leptomeninges or CNS
next_steps: Send serum and CSF for autoimmune and paraneoplastic antibody panels (including LGI1, CASPR2, Hu, Ma2)
Perform whole-body PET/CT scan to screen for occult malignancy
Initiate empiric high-dose intravenous corticosteroid therapy for presumed inflammatory/autoimmune process
Continue antiseizure therapy with levetiracetam or lacosamide
Brain biopsy if non-invasive workup is nondiagnostic and clinical suspicion for vasculitis or malignancy remains high

differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune limbic encephalitis (LGI1 or other VGKC-complex antibody)
Neurodegenerative disease with superimposed cerebrovascular insult
next_steps: Perform serum and CSF testing for comprehensive autoimmune and paraneoplastic antibody panels
Initiate empiric high-dose intravenous corticosteroid therapy for suspected inflammatory limbic encephalitis
Obtain whole-body FDG-PET/CT scan to screen for occult malignancy
Send CSF for cytology and flow cytometry to rule out malignancy
Continue antiseizure therapy with lacosamide to manage temporal lobe epileptiform activity
Perform close review of prior imaging to re-evaluate the stability of the lung nodule
